# Version 4.151.0 (Unreleased)
> **Performance and stability**

## Highlights

SkiaSharp 4.151.0 brings allocation-free hot paths, span-friendly APIs, and m151 stability fixes. Two performance improvements eliminate per-call allocations in `SKFourByteTag.Parse` and `SKColor` hex parsing. The bundled engine folds in two rounds of Skia m151 upstream bug fixes, and `GRContextOptions` drops its unnecessary unsafe modifier.

## Breaking Changes

*None in this preview line.*

## Engine

- **Skia m151 upstream bug fixes (two syncs)** — Two rounds of upstream chrome/m151 bug-fix merges land in this line, including the SubRunAllocator use-after-free fix that improves GPU text rendering stability. ([#4312](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4312), [#4382](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4382))

## API Surface

- **SKColor parsing is faster and span-friendly** — Parse and TryParse now use an allocation-free hex parser and add ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads, so callers can parse colors from larger buffers without slicing strings. ([#4345](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4345))
- **GRContextOptions no longer requires unsafe context** — The type no longer carries an unsafe modifier, allowing projects to configure GPU contexts without enabling unsafe code just to reference the options class. ([#3991](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/3991))

## Bug Fixes

- **Allocation eliminated in SKFourByteTag.Parse** — The per-call char[4] allocation in SKFourByteTag.Parse is removed, reducing pressure in hot parsing paths. ([#4396](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4396))

## Community Contributors ❤️

Thank you to everyone who contributed to this release!
